module_role: Module 6 of v17 integration — faction-unique action expansion. Crown Initiative 3 modes + faction analogues (Synod, Charter, Vaynard's Hall) + Church Excommunication + Church Absolution + RM Cultural Uprising of T9 + parametric tactic card pool modifiers deferred from M3. Pure primitives — computes pool/Ob/outcome dicts; does not mutate state.

entries.quoted_text: Faction | Crown (Valorsmark) only
Slot | Senator Inward (new) — 1x per season; shares no slot with Senator Outward (Crown Treaty) or other cards
Modes | Three; player declares mode at play. Mode I - Royal Progress; Mode II - Great Work; Mode III - Coronation Renewal.
Cost | Wealth -2 (treasury for entertainment, gifts to hosts)

entries.section: §3.2 Mode I — Royal Progress Ob row
entries.quoted_text: Ob | floor(sum of own territories Accord / 2) — easier when Accord is high (people aren't restive); harder when realm is in turmoil

entries.section: §3.2 Mode I — Royal Progress Pool row
entries.quoted_text: Pool | Influence + Standing modifier
Overwhelming | Mandate +2; Standing +1; all own territories at Accord 1 -> Accord 2
Success | Mandate +1; one own territory of choice (lowest Accord) +1 Accord
Partial | Standing +1 (tour happened, didn't move the needle); cost still paid
Failure | Standing -1 (no welcome at one stop); cost paid; no L gain
Cost | Wealth -1 per season for 3 seasons (sustained investment) AND uses Senator Inward slot each season
I will undertake [codification / monument / charter] over 3 seasons

entries.section: §3.3 Mode II — Great Work Ob row
entries.quoted_text: Ob (final season) | 4 — challenging; major project

entries.section: §3.3 Mode II — Great Work Pool row
entries.quoted_text: Pool (final season) | Mandate (the institutional weight backing the project)
Overwhelming | Mandate +2 permanent; all own Charters gain Govern -1 Ob (permanent quality-of-life), Standing +2

entries.section: §3.3 Great Work outcome table + Mid-pledge breach row
entries.quoted_text: Failure | Standing -2, Cohesion -20 (per PP-515 breach penalties); the project failed publicly. Mid-pledge breach | Standing -2, Cohesion -20, Reputation -15 (PP-515)
Cost | Wealth -2 (ceremony, gifts to Church, hosting clergy)

entries.value: valid relations: truce/peace/alliance/crown_treaty; blocks if Excomm active
entries.quoted_text: Prerequisites | Crown-Church active Truce, Peace, Alliance, or Crown Treaty; Church is not actively prosecuting an Excommunication against Crown this season

entries.section: §3.4 Mode III — Coronation Renewal Ob row
entries.quoted_text: Ob | floor(Church Mandate / 2) + 1

entries.section: §3.4 Mode III — Coronation Renewal outcome table
entries.quoted_text: Overwhelming | Mandate +2; if Crown currently Excommunicated, Excommunication lifted; Standing +1; Crown-Church Standing +1

entries.value: Mode III Success also lifts Excommunication
entries.section: §3.4 Mode III — Coronation Renewal outcome Success row + §6.4
entries.quoted_text: Success | Mandate +1; Excommunication lifted if currently active. §6.4: Mode III as the canonical Excommunication-recovery path.

entries.section: Church sheet — Tactic / Unique Actions: Excommunication
entries.quoted_text: Requires Church L >= 3. Reversal: Grand Debate (5 exchanges) or new Confessor.
Church | Excommunication | L vs target L (leader) / Ob 2 (non-leader) | Requires Church L >= 3
Overwhelming | Strips target's Circles bonus with Church contacts; target faction L -1; target barred from public office and Church-loyal command; personal Reputation -1 with all factions

entries.value: as OW minus the personal Reputation penalty
entries.quoted_text: Success | As Overwhelming minus the personal Reputation penalty
Failure | Church L -1; target gains L +1 (sympathy martyr)
Church Absolution (Church unique action): +1 to target.
Canon specifies +1 Stability effect. Pool/Ob/cost not specified; M6 declares Ob=3 (mid-tier sacrament difficulty) — M6_ASSUMPTION_ONE
entries.assumption: M6_ASSUMPTION_ONE: Ob 3 declared by M6; not in canon. Surface for ratification.

entries.quoted_text: Cost not specified in canon; M6 declares Mandate -1 (Church spends institutional capital absolving) — M6_ASSUMPTION_ONE
Ob | floor(CI / 30) + 2 (easier when CI is high — Church has institutional momentum)
Effects (OW) | Church Mandate +2; choose a Cardinal Focus permanent + select one rival faction's L -> -1 (formal censure)
Ob | 4
Pool | Influence + (active Tokens on opponents)
Effects (OW) | Hafenmark Mandate +2; PI -2; all rival factions face +1 Ob to Excommunication attempts this campaign (constitutional protection)
Ob | 3
Pool | Mil + (Tribune Network active)
PT <= 1 in >= 4 of the 15 playable territories (T1-T14, T17) (PP-543)
PT <= 1 in >= 4 of the 15 playable territories (PP-543: was >= 8; PP-478 had >= 5 — PP-543 supersedes both)

entries.section: §3.5 Phase 2 — Cultural Uprising of T9 Himmelenger
entries.quoted_text: Prerequisites: MS >= 25 (substrate must be stable enough for a coherent popular movement — below 25, physical destabilisation overwhelms cultural organisation). If MS < 25, Cultural Uprising is unavailable regardless of Phase 1 status.

entries.value: max(1, min(5, ceil(ci/10))) + modifiers (T9 PT<=1: -1; CI>=50: +1; Mandate>=5: +1)
entries.section: §3.5 Phase 2 — Cultural Uprising of T9 Himmelenger
entries.quoted_text: Weaver Thread pool vs Ob = CI / 10 (round up, min 1, max 5). Modifiers: T9 PT <= 1: Ob -1 (Cathedral already culturally shifted); WC >= 2: +1D (Wardens support the Uprising); CI >= 50 at declaration: Ob +1 (Church institutional authority is strong enough to resist); Church Mandate >= 5: Ob +1 (Church actively suppresses)

entries.section: §3.5 Phase 2 outcome table + Overwhelming Uprising bonus
entries.quoted_text: Overwhelming | T9 transfers to RM administration. Church Mandate -2. CI -3 (institutional rupture). T9 PT -2 (population has decisively shifted). Overwhelming Uprising bonus: +2 Presence markers in T9.
Failure | Uprising crushed. CI +2 (Church authority strengthened by resistance). T9 PT +1. Uprising attempt used up for this arc.

entries.value: 9 parametric cards: royal_guard, mercenary_surge, sovereign_authority, paid_off, logistics_mastery, assassination, iron_discipline, martial_law, peoples_courage, ambush
entries.quoted_text: Crown | Royal Guard (Elite unit +3D); Hafenmark | Mercenary Surge (pay 1 Wealth: +2 units this engagement) | Sovereign Authority (immune to Disposition table Ob penalties this engagement); Guilds | Paid Off (opponent unit -1D; costs 1 Wealth) | Logistics Mastery (Strained units fight at full this engagement); Niflhel | Assassination (target opponent commander; -1D all opp. units); Löwenritter | Iron Discipline (immune to Route this engagement) | Martial Law (after winning: territory gains Martial Law next season); Revolution | People's Courage (Discipline +1 all units this engagement) | Ambush (first engagement in Oastad or Stillhelm: opponent no Defence roll)

Stratagem (read opponent's tactic card before locking yours) — 2-pass init inversion; Crusade Fervour (Discipline check exempt this turn) — overrides check_route; Inquisitor's Mark (target unit -2D) — per-unit targeting; Calculated Retreat (withdraw without Overextended penalty) — outcome override; Disappear (withdraw all units; opponent cannot pursue this season) — outcome override; Ducal Call (summon 1 unit from adjacent territory) — resolution-time state mutation.
entries.note: M6_ASSUMPTION_THREE: these 6 cards require resolution-time hooks not present in M6 primitives layer. M6's tactic_card_effect() raises NotImplementedError on these; M7 integration wires post-resolution callbacks.

entries.quoted_text: Outcome degree set per mc_v15 sim convention + victory_v30 outcome tables: Overwhelming / Success / Partial / Failure.

provisional_assumptions.decision: Canon says 'Church Absolution (Church unique action): +1 Stability to target' in §8.2 but does not specify Pool, Ob, or cost. M6 declares: Pool = Church Influence; Ob = 3; cost = Church Mandate -1; Frequency: 1/season. Failure path: Church Standing -1.
provisional_assumptions.rationale: +1 Stability is the most powerful single-action recovery in the game (per §8.2 it's the same magnitude as 'Mutual peace treaty' which requires both parties at Stability >=2). Pool=Influence matches the diplomatic-sacred frame. Ob=3 is mid-tier (matching Crown Treaty defensive average). Mandate cost reflects 'absolving target costs Church institutional capital' historical pattern (papacy taking on the disgrace of pardoning a heretic).
provisional_assumptions.follow_up: Jordan ratification required. Could alternatively be Pool=Mandate (the absolver's institutional weight), Ob=floor(target_disgrace/2)+1, or other formulations. Compare to Coronation Renewal Ob = floor(Church Mandate / 2) + 1 — Absolution is a symmetric-relationship-affirming action so a flat Ob seems right.

provisional_assumptions.topic: Standing as integer modifier in outcome dicts
provisional_assumptions.decision: All M6 outcome dicts use 'standing_delta' as an integer. Crown-Church relationship Standing uses 'crown_church_standing_delta'.
provisional_assumptions.rationale: Canon (Part 10 §3.2-§3.4) refers to 'Standing +1' / 'Standing -1' as integer modifiers without specifying the underlying stat structure (Standing-as-stat vs Standing-as-track). M6 stays agnostic — caller integrates with whatever Standing tracking mechanism eventually lands in canon.
provisional_assumptions.follow_up: When Standing is fully canonized (likely npc_behavior_v30 or faction_state_authoring extensions), revisit whether M6 should clamp Standing deltas to a range or pass through unbounded.

provisional_assumptions.topic: Faction-specific tactic card effects — primitives vs resolution-time hooks
provisional_assumptions.decision: M6 implements 10 cards with constant pool modifiers as parametric effect dicts (Royal Guard +3D, Mercenary Surge +2 costing W, Sovereign Authority immune-to-Ob-penalties, Paid Off -1D, Logistics Mastery strained-at-full, Assassination -1D, Iron Discipline immune-to-route, Martial Law martial-law-next-season-on-win, People's Courage +1 Discipline, Ambush no-defence-in-specific-territories). M6 raises NotImplementedError for 6 cards requiring resolution-time hooks (Stratagem 2-pass, Crusade Fervour check-route override, Inquisitor's Mark per-unit targeting, Calculated Retreat outcome override, Disappear outcome override, Ducal Call resolution-time state mutation).
provisional_assumptions.rationale: Primitives-vs-hooks split keeps M6 self-contained. The 6 deferred cards genuinely require either (a) two-pass tactic resolution (Stratagem), (b) overriding M3's check_route function (Crusade Fervour), (c) per-unit targeting not present in M4's aggregate-count model (Inquisitor's Mark), or (d) post-resolution state mutation beyond what BattleResult expresses (Calculated Retreat / Disappear / Ducal Call). M7 integration is the right home.
provisional_assumptions.follow_up: M7 must wire a post-resolution-callback architecture. Specifically: (1) Stratagem requires re-running apply_tactic_cards once defender's card is revealed; (2) Crusade Fervour suppresses the check_route call in M3 for that faction's units this engagement; (3) Inquisitor's Mark requires extending damage allocation to target a specific unit class first; (4) Disappear/Calculated Retreat short-circuit the outcome to a withdrawal state not currently in BattleResult; (5) Ducal Call adds a pre-resolution Muster-from-adjacent step.

provisional_assumptions.topic: RM Cultural Uprising 'Weaver Thread pool' source
provisional_assumptions.decision: uprising_pool_modifier() returns +1 if WC >= 2 (canon explicit). The base 'Weaver Thread pool' value is taken as a precomputed integer parameter by the caller — M6 does not compute Weaver Thread aggregation.
provisional_assumptions.rationale: The 'Weaver Thread pool' canonical aggregation lives in another doc (likely npc_behavior_v30 or thread mechanics layer, not yet read this session). M6 stays focused on action-level mechanics; the caller (M7 integration) computes Weaver Thread aggregation and passes the result.
provisional_assumptions.follow_up: Read the Weaver Thread aggregation rules when M7 wires the full action; M6 can be extended to compute it if needed, or M7 owns the computation.

provisional_assumptions.topic: Outcome degree semantics across all M6 actions
provisional_assumptions.decision: DEGREES = ('Overwhelming', 'Success', 'Partial', 'Failure'). Every M6 *_outcome() function accepts a degree string from this set; unknown degrees raise ValueError. Partial/Failure degrees that canon doesn't explicitly define for an action (e.g. Excommunication Partial) return either {} (no effect, cost paid) or a documented minimum-impact dict.
provisional_assumptions.rationale: Matches mc_v15 sim convention and victory_v30 outcome tables. Most actions in canon define OW/Success/Partial/Failure explicitly; Excommunication only defines OW/Success/Failure. M6's default for missing degrees is 'cost paid, no effect' — the most conservative interpretation.
provisional_assumptions.follow_up: If canon adds Partial cases for actions M6 currently defaults to {} (notably Excommunication Partial, Council Partial/Failure, Charter Partial/Failure, Vaynard's Hall Partial/Failure), update those branches.
